title: Releases | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## Mental health response for Tauranga 111 calls
Tauranga people experiencing mental distress will receive support from a new mental health co-response team, Mental Health Minister Matt Doocey announced today.

## Government approves new plan to eliminate bovine TB
The Government, in partnership with the beef, dairy and deer industries, plans to knock out tuberculosis (TB)-infected possums in certain ‘hot spot’ parts of the country to protect herds.

## Minister to represent New Zealand at key labour conference
Workplace Relations and Safety Minister and Internal Affairs Minister Brooke van Velden will travel to Geneva this month where she will represent New Zealand at the International Labour Conference.

## Improving services for disabled people and carers
Disabled people and their carers will see further improvements to disability support services with a new community consultation, and work about to get underway to provide better support for family carers.

## More trades academy places for young people
More young New Zealanders will have the opportunity to gain practical skills and industry experience while still at school, with the Government doubling the number of trades academy places available.

## Council voting to be restricted to elected members
Only elected councillors will be able to vote on council committees, in a move that strengthens democratic accountability, Local Government Minister Simon Watts announced today.

## Marsden Point Rail Link progresses
Minister for Rail Winston Peters has welcomed KiwiRail identification of three potential construction partners for delivering the ‘design sprint’ needed for the Government to proceed with funding a cost-effective Marsden Point Rail Link.

## Workable lending laws delivered
The Credit Contracts and Consumer Finance Amendment Bill passed its third reading last week, delivering simpler, clearer, and more workable lending laws for New Zealanders, Commerce and Consumer Affairs Minister

## Deep South’s mega marine life gets historic new protections
Some of New Zealand’s most remarkable marine environments and species are set to benefit from significant new protections along the Otago coast, Conservation Minister Tama Potaka says.

## New social homes for autistic people
Housing Minister Chris Bishop welcomes the opening of seven new social homes in Tauranga designed specifically for autistic people.
